beats.activity: Wakes up, coffee, tends to houseplants (including newly named 'Houdini' the pothos).
beats.reasoning: Full energy day starting; sunny weather lifts mood. Ritual coffee and plant care grounds the morning before work commute.

beats.activity: Drives to SoDo library branch; Light Rail closure does not affect his car commute.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library – SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Sand Point to SoDo is a cross-town drive (~20 min); he avoids Light Rail today anyway due to 9am–3pm closure, so car is natural choice.

beats.activity: Morning shift at the library: cataloging, helping patrons, desk work.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library – SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Standard library opening; work until the 11am FIFA match window. Cyrus is skeptical but SoDo is close to downtown bars if he wants to catch the match.

beats.activity: Walks to a nearby sports bar to watch USA vs Wales FIFA match kickoff.
beats.reasoning: SoDo has several bars; this is a rare competitive soccer moment and Cyrus's competitive nature means he won't miss it. Match starts at 11am sharp—he catches the opening.

beats.activity: Watches USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup group stage match; orders lunch.
beats.reasoning: Match runs ~2 hours; Cyrus is competitive and won't miss a live match. Combines leisure and meal in one location.

beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library – SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Short walk back; Light Rail closure ends at 3pm, so no impact on his afternoon schedule.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: returns to desk, manages reference desk, helps patrons with research queries.
beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library – SoDo Branch (SoDo, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Completes his work day. Light Rail closure lifted by now; he's still at work so not affected.

beats.activity: Drives home from SoDo to Sand Point.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute; sunny day, moderate traffic. Returns home by early evening.

beats.activity: Prepares a light dinner; eats at home while listening to a chess podcast.
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening meal; competitive energy from the morning match fades into introspective leisure. Knitting or chess would follow naturally.

beats.activity: Works on a knitting project (a sweater in progress); plays online chess against a friend.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus values close friendships and solo hobbies; perfect wind-down after a full day. Sunny day and match energy offset; settling into evening rhythm.

diary: Woke up early to tend my plants—Houdini's looking particularly perky this morning, which felt like a good omen. The drive to SoDo was smooth, the sun already warm through the windshield. Spent the morning doing the usual cataloging work, helping a regular track down some obscure Pacific Northwest history books. Around eleven, I couldn't resist—walked over to Pyramid Tavern to catch the USA vs Wales match. Something about a Monday morning in a bar with other enthusiasts, cold drink in hand, felt oddly perfect. The game was tense, the energy electric. Grabbed a sandwich and just sat there, letting the day slow down. Back at the library by afternoon, the reference desk kept me busy with research queries, but I was still riding that match-day buzz. Home now, chess podcast in my ears, needles clicking away on this sweater. Quiet evening. Content.
thought_process: I knew the Light Rail closure wouldn't affect me—driving has its perks—so I didn't feel the usual commute friction that might plague others today. The World Cup match really pulled me in though; at fifty, I've learned that spontaneity still matters, even on a workday. I could've eaten at my desk, but instead I took the excuse to step outside, be around people, feel the energy of something bigger than my regular routines. It reset my afternoon. By evening, the knitting and chess felt like the natural rhythm of a full day—work, play, work, then home rituals. The weather helped too; seventy-four degrees in early June is Seattle showing off, and I wasn't going to waste it stuck indoors.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
